campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's money was invested and spent that month. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as adding bus routes, bike lanes, or pedestrian walkways.
keyTopics: July 2025 financial review and investment performance
City treasurer's monthly budget report
Campbell's multimodal transportation plan (buses, bikes, walking)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and scheduled it for final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced (specific topic not detailed in agenda)
Fast-tracked approval process used to expedite the rule
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: Los Altos City Council met to wrap up their 2026 weed control program, establish new rules for how council meetings will operate, and address affordable housing policies. The council also approved raising two flags to celebrate Pride Month and Juneteenth, and heard from the regional transportation authority about transit planning.
keyTopics: Completed 2026 weed abatement program - final report adopted
New council meeting rules and procedures established
Affordable housing policy: exemptions for below-market-rate unit transfers
Pride Month and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Regional transit planning presentation and housing implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with town projects and services. Key decisions included hiring QLM, Inc. for construction work, bringing in Dudek for professional services, and joining a statewide insurance pool (CJPIA) to potentially save money on insurance costs. The council also authorized updates to existing agreements with other service providers.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Contract amendments with NHA and other vendors
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed how well the city is serving its young people, including updates on youth programs and summer safety initiatives. The council also heard reports on code enforcement activities and discussed plans to improve library facilities and customer service across the city.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety grant programs
Youth intervention services and annual performance results
Code enforcement compliance and violations
Library building improvements and visitor experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in-person or online via Zoom. Residents who wanted to speak during public comment could join the virtual meeting and use the 'raise hand' feature to be called on by the council chair.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents
Public comment procedures for online participants
Phone dial-in option (669-900-9128) for those without internet

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official record of their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and job opportunities for young people during the summer. The council also approved meeting minutes from the previous month and opened the floor for residents to comment on any local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer job opportunities for young people
Public comment period for residents
Approval of March 11 meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person attendance and remote options so residents could participate however works best for them. The city is making it easy for people to join and share their opinions, either by showing up in person or joining online through Zoom.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join online via Zoom
Remote participation options available by phone
Public comment submission through eComments system on City Council Agenda page
Multiple ways for residents to engage with city government

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and infrastructure matters. Key items included joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a community education organization, awarding a construction contract, and reviewing housing/community development plans.
keyTopics: Partnership with Franchise Tax Board to improve city-county coordination
Fee waiver request from Milpitas Community Educational organization
Construction project approval and contract award
Updates to housing and community development plans (2025-2030)
New workplan requests from individual council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for 2027. The main decision involved reviewing the financial forecast for the gas utility, moving money between accounts, and adjusting how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service.
keyTopics: 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast approval
Gas rate changes for residential customers (G-1 rate schedule)
Gas rate changes for commercial and master-metered properties (G-2 and G-3)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund money
